_meta.description: D6 fixtures for hermes / tool-rendering-custom-catchall. SERVER-SIDE backend tools (Hermes `hermes-showcase` toolset, integrations/hermes/showcase_tools.py), 1:1 with langgraph-python; rendered by a SINGLE custom wildcard renderer (useDefaultRenderTool with CustomCatchallRenderer → [data-testid='custom-wildcard-card'][data-tool-name='<tool>']). The fixture only makes the agent EMIT the tool call (leg 1, gated on toolName); Hermes runs the REAL handler in-loop (result via TOOL_CALL_RESULT), then the narration leg (gated on toolCallId, ordered first) fires. The tool RESULT comes from the server handler, NOT the fixture. Two-leg toolCallId pattern (2nd leg above 1st). D5 probe sends two DISJOINT prompts ('Forecast Tokyo through the wildcard renderer' → get_weather, 'Quote AAPL through the wildcard renderer' → get_stock_price) and asserts BOTH render through the same testid AND the narration contains the canonical phrase 'rendered through the custom wildcard catchall' (LGP-gold disjoint-prompts content guard). Pills (verbatim from suggestions.ts): "What's the weather in San Francisco?", "Find flights from SFO to JFK.", "Roll a 20-sided die.", "Chain a few tools in this single turn: ...".

fixtures._comment: D5 probe turn 1 — narration after get_weather tool result. Scoped via toolCallId; ordered BEFORE the tool-emit sibling. Canonical phrase 'rendered through the custom wildcard catchall' asserted by the probe.

match.userMessage: Forecast Tokyo through the wildcard renderer

response.content: Tokyo is 68°F and sunny — the custom wildcard renderer shows the tool card above — rendered through the custom wildcard catchall.

fixtures._comment: D5 probe turn 1 — emit get_weather (Tokyo). Disjoint userMessage from the default-catchall probe ('forecast for Tokyo' lowercase) so aimock's substring matcher cannot cross-route between catchall probes.

match.userMessage: Forecast Tokyo through the wildcard renderer

fixtures._comment: D5 probe turn 2 — narration after get_stock_price tool result. Scoped via toolCallId. Canonical phrase asserted by the probe.

match.userMessage: Quote AAPL through the wildcard renderer

response.content: AAPL is trading at $338.37, down 2.96% — rendered through the custom wildcard catchall renderer.

fixtures._comment: D5 probe turn 2 — emit get_stock_price (AAPL). Cross-tool pair with get_weather; the probe asserts both tools render through the SAME custom-wildcard-card testid.

match.userMessage: Quote AAPL through the wildcard renderer

fixtures._comment: Chain tools — follow-up after all 3 tools ran (last chain toolCallId).

match.userMessage: Chain a few tools in this single turn

response.content: Done — Tokyo is sunny, three flights found, and the d20 came up 11.

match.userMessage: Chain a few tools in this single turn

response.content: Done — Tokyo is sunny, three flights found, and the d20 came up 11.

match.userMessage: Chain a few tools in this single turn

response.content: Done — Tokyo is sunny, three flights found, and the d20 came up 11.

match.userMessage: Chain a few tools in this single turn
What's the weather in San Francisco?

response.content: San Francisco is currently 68°F and sunny with light winds — rendered through the custom wildcard catchall.

match.userMessage: What's the weather in San Francisco?
Find flights from SFO to JFK.

response.content: Three flights from SFO to JFK — United UA231 at 08:15 ($348), Delta DL412 at 11:20 ($312), and JetBlue B6722 at 17:05 ($289).

match.userMessage: Find flights from SFO to JFK.

fixtures._comment: Roll a d20 — 5 sequential rolls [7,14,3,19,20], chained by toolCallId.

response.content: Rolled the d20 five times — landed on 20 on the final roll.
